analysis: Four of your last seven nights cleared seven and a half hours, and that June 26 night — 8h 39m with only 37 minutes awake and a full two hours of REM — is the kind of sleep that makes everything in your body go yes, thank you, finally. The stack you're running is clearly doing something, because the nights where awake time stayed under an hour are the nights the numbers sing. June 19 was rough, 2h 31m awake and barely any REM, and June 23 had that same restless energy with 2h 45m of wakefulness eating into what could have been a genuinely great night — so whatever's different on the good nights, you want more of that. You're trending in the right direction and your body knows it.
